ir_specs.statement: The `nice invocation` -n option Add adjustment instead of 10 to the command’s niceness.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Add adjustment instead of 10 to the command’s niceness.

ir_specs.statement: nice prints a process’s niceness , or runs a command with modified niceness.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: nice prints a process’s niceness , or runs a command with modified niceness.

ir_specs.statement: niceness affects how favorably the process is scheduled in the system.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: niceness affects how favorably the process is scheduled in the system.

ir_specs.statement: If no arguments are given, nice prints the current niceness.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If no arguments are given, nice prints the current niceness.

ir_specs.statement: Otherwise, nice runs the given command with its niceness adjusted.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Otherwise, nice runs the given command with its niceness adjusted.

ir_specs.statement: By default, its niceness is incremented by 10.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: By default, its niceness is incremented by 10.

ir_specs.statement: Some systems may have a wider range of niceness values; conversely, other systems may enforce more restrictive limits.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Some systems may have a wider range of niceness values; conversely, other systems may enforce more restrictive limits.

ir_specs.statement: An attempt to set the niceness outside the supported range is treated as an attempt to use the minimum or maximum supported value.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: An attempt to set the niceness outside the supported range is treated as an attempt to use the minimum or maximum supported value.

ir_specs.statement: A niceness should not be confused with a scheduling priority, which lets applications determine the order in which threads are scheduled to run.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: A niceness should not be confused with a scheduling priority, which lets applications determine the order in which threads are scheduled to run.

ir_specs.statement: Unlike a priority, a niceness is merely advice to the scheduler, which the scheduler is free to ignore.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Unlike a priority, a niceness is merely advice to the scheduler, which the scheduler is free to ignore.

ir_specs.statement: Also, as a point of terminology, POSIX defines the behavior of nice in terms of a nice value , which is the non-negative difference between a niceness and the minimum niceness.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Also, as a point of terminology, POSIX defines the behavior of nice in terms of a nice value , which is the non-negative difference between a niceness and the minimum niceness.

ir_specs.statement: Though nice conforms to POSIX, its documentation and diagnostics use the term “niceness” for compatibility with historical practice.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Though nice conforms to POSIX, its documentation and diagnostics use the term “niceness” for compatibility with historical practice.

ir_specs.statement: command must not be a special built-in utility (see Special built-in utilities ).
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: command must not be a special built-in utility (see Special built-in utilities ).

ir_specs.statement: Due to shell aliases and built-in nice functions, using an unadorned nice interactively or in a script may get you different functionality than that described here.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Due to shell aliases and built-in nice functions, using an unadorned nice interactively or in a script may get you different functionality than that described here.

ir_specs.statement: Invoke it via env (i.e., env nice … ) to avoid interference from the shell.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Invoke it via env (i.e., env nice … ) to avoid interference from the shell.
